mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance improves.
failure_condition: Payroll growth > 200k does not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends earnings downgrades and risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction or post-shock demand collapse
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS and HOUST contractions do not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerating above 2% year-over-year signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 2.0% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: INDPRO growth > 2% does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, triggering a negative feedback loop that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates decline.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.5% and remains elevated for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 62% of instances, or the relationship reverses sign in more than 40% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Joint condition of high rates and rising inflation does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 65% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 1,600 thousand units annualized signals robust residential investment and forward consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as construction and related equity sectors outperform.
candidate_trigger: HOUST exceeds 1,600 thousand units for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: housing cycle expansion or post-trough recovery
failure_condition: HOUST > 1,600k does not produce positive 5-day returns in at least 56% of instances, or correlation with 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declining while unemployment rises simultaneously signals stagflationary pressure that erodes profit margins and increases equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in same month
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise do not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 68% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression.
